angle: Market-dominance: PLAT-005 pattern-interrupt + recursion (the category ran one way, now that platform raises)
hook_type: The registered dominance number as a complete sentence, full stop, recursion turn
tactic: Dominance pattern-interrupt hook -> what the platform has been -> it is opening its own offer -> waitlist
primary_text:
More than 64% of Australian crowd-sourced funding ran one way. Now that platform raises.

For eight years it has been where Australians back the companies they believe in, not where venture funds do. Now the platform is opening its own crowd-sourced funding offer.

You can own a piece of it. You read the offer document first, the day it goes live.

Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: Where over 64% of Australian CSF went
description: More than 64% of Australian CSF ran one way. Leave your email.
_source_label: [64% CITATION PENDING] DRC V5c -- DOM3 Market-dominance / PLAT-005 pattern-interrupt (existing investors)
_iterates: BIRCHAL-V5-W set (waitlist regime). New V5c structure: 4 angles x 4 hook-distinct variants under learnings-meta-ad.md #5 + Generation prompt addendum (one idea per sentence, ONE number then a turn, reader is subject at the turn and CTA, literal email micro-action CTA per WAITLIST-CTA 2026-05-18).
_v5c_rationale: One of 4 hook-distinct variants in its angle; the hook (first 1-2 sentences) is the primary differentiator, body adapted to fit the hook (JB-permitted 2026-05-18).

Built to the learnings-meta-ad.md #5 / Generation prompt addendum 8 structural gates: opens with ONE verified number (or Caples Why on a verified number) as a complete sentence, full stop, then a turn; the reader is the grammatical subject at the turn and the CTA; no third-party company names in the bridge; plain concrete nouns; one proof point per beat; CTA = literal email micro-action with the exchange; verbatim CSF warning byte-exact terminator.

CTA field is the WAITLIST-CTA approved verbatim 'Save my spot' (landing-page canonical button); the in-copy close uses the 2026-05-18 JB-preferred literal email micro-action 'Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live' (FMTH top-performer pattern, CSF-clean notification framing).
Strict WAITLIST regime: Birchal's own CSF offer is not live until 26 June 2026 (OFFER-CONTEXT). No transaction verbs, no $ minimum, no preferential-terms framing. Pre-offer compliant.
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who already know the platform's standing; recursion is the lever.
_a_pile_signals: specific number in first 8 words (more than 64%)
_compliance_findings_resolved: PLAT-005 usage: used ONCE, registered scoped wording 'More than 64% of Australian crowd-sourced funding' (scope inline in the hook sentence itself, not reliant on the headline), never with #1/first/largest. Paired with verified spine (eight years). source_status OPEN: DRAFT/JB-review only, not marked live until citation attached. Post-verify 2026-05-18: headline + description rescoped to carry 'Australian CSF' inline (adversarial-verify defects #1/#2) so the Meta-independently-rotating fields are scoped, not only the primary_text.
PREOFFER-VERBS: 'Leave your email', 'we will tell you the moment', 'read the offer document first'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.
No OTHER market-share / #1 / largest / leading / first. 'not where venture funds do' is a qualitative spine characterisation, not a numeric claim. Single share figure only.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' zero times; 'that platform' / 'the platform' resolved with first-person 'its own crowd-sourced funding offer'.
Number traceability: more than 64%=PLAT-005 (citation-pending; scope established by surrounding copy), eight years=PLAT-004.
